raw_consumer_statement:
My Wayfair credit card was subject to fraudulent activity on or around XX/XX/XXXX, upon discovering the fraud via a suspicious delivery confirmation text, I immediately contacted Wayfair credit customer services. At the time of reporting, the account had not been used in over a year and held a XXXX balance. An agent from Wayfair confirmed some of the fraudulent transactions had been approved and a few others were still pending. The agent informed me they were going to close my account immediately and then open a new one. 

Despite this, I was shocked to later see a balance of {$1300.00} transferred to my new account. The {$1300.00} comprised of fraudulent charges that were allowed to go through after the original account was supposedly closed. Over the next several months, I engaged in over XXXX documented phone calls, most of them lasting more than an hour, attempting to explain the situation again and again to agents and supervisors, who repeatedly failed to properly document or escalate my case. 

While I made good faith minimum payments totalling {$400.00} between XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX to protect my credit score, Wayfair Credit not only erroneously held me responsible for fraudulent charges but also accrued interest at predatory rates ( 29.99 % -32.24 % ). After nearly a year of delay and stress, the case was finally resolved and fraudulent balance cleared. A check of {$170.00} was issued to myself leaving me with an out of pocket loss of {$220.00} not including interest.
